cases.infamy: the state of being well known for a bad quality or deed
cases.palpable: so intense as to be almost tangible, easily perceived
cases.presumptuous: overstepping what is appropriate, acting with unwarranted boldness
cases.self-effacing: modest, not drawing attention to oneself
cases.duress: threats or force used to compel someone to act against their will
cases.crass: lacking sensitivity or refinement, coarse and unrefined
cases.acerbic: sharp and forthright in a biting, unpleasant way
cases.dilapidated: in a state of disrepair from age or neglect
cases.ransack: to search a place thoroughly, often causing disorder or damage
cases.conduit: a channel through which something is transmitted, or a pipe for liquid
cases.exorcise: to drive out an evil spirit, or figuratively to rid oneself of something troubling
cases.retort: to reply to something quickly, sharply, or wittily
cases.elicit: to draw out a response or reaction from someone
cases.solace: comfort in a time of distress or sadness
cases.nihilistic: believing that life is meaningless and rejecting all moral principles
cases.artifice: clever trickery, especially used to deceive
cases.feisty: spirited and full of determination, especially in a combative way
cases.swindle: to cheat someone out of money or property
cases.kowtow: to act in an excessively subservient manner
cases.bated breath: in a state of anxious or excited anticipation
cases.notoriety: the state of being famous for something bad
cases.cynicism: a distrustful attitude that assumes people are motivated by self-interest
cases.flamboyant: showy and confident in a colorful, extravagant way
cases.gluttonous: having an excessive appetite, greedy for food or indulgence
cases.amok: in an uncontrolled or frenzied way
cases.deceitful: guilty of or involving deceit, dishonest
cases.forte: a thing at which someone excels
cases.rhetoric: language designed to persuade or impress, sometimes lacking substance
cases.misogynistic: showing a strong prejudice against women
cases.fawn over: to flatter someone excessively or show exaggerated affection
cases.pander: to indulge or gratify someone's desires or weaknesses
cases.ostensibly: as appears to be true, though not necessarily so
cases.ostracize: to exclude someone from a group deliberately
cases.serenade: to sing or play music for someone, especially as a romantic gesture
cases.carnal: relating to physical or sexual desires
cases.abstinence: the practice of restraining oneself from indulgence, especially of desire
cases.necrotic: relating to the death of body tissue
cases.echelon: a level or rank in an organization or society
cases.inextricable: impossible to disentangle or escape from
cases.imperturbable: unable to be upset or excited, calm
cases.tutelage: guidance and instruction given by a mentor
cases.nuzzle: to gently push or rub against something affectionately with the nose
cases.accomplice: a person who helps another commit a crime
cases.invigorate: to give someone energy and enthusiasm
cases.surreptitious: done secretly to avoid being noticed
cases.smarmy: excessively flattering in an insincere way
cases.pandemonium: wild and noisy disorder or confusion
cases.vendetta: a long-lasting bitter feud or grudge
cases.factoid: a brief, often trivial piece of information
cases.flounder: to struggle to move or think, to act clumsily in confusion
cases.blithe: showing a casual lack of concern, carefree
cases.wishy-washy: lacking decisiveness or strength of character
cases.ambidextrous: able to use both hands equally well
cases.compunction: a feeling of guilt or moral hesitation
cases.eloquent: fluent and persuasive in speaking or writing
cases.consummate: showing a high degree of skill, complete and perfect
cases.satiate: to satisfy a desire or appetite fully
cases.snub: to rebuff or ignore someone disdainfully
cases.affront: an action or remark that offends someone
cases.scuttle: to move hurriedly with short quick steps
cases.hubbub: a loud confused noise from a crowd of people
